---
title: "Managing an Evaluation"
description: "Study note on Managing an Evaluation in Magenta Book."
book_id: "magenta-book"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "8"
citation_key: "hm_treasury_evaluation_task_force_2020_magenta_book"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:magenta-book:magenta-book-5-managing-an-evaluation:c0017"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2020</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Source pages 8</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "Managing an Evaluation" from HM Treasury and Evaluation Task Force's *Magenta Book: Central Government guidance on evaluation* [@hm_treasury_evaluation_task_force_2020_magenta_book]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

The management section highlights the balance between consistency and flexibility.

## Study Summary

The management section highlights the balance between consistency and flexibility.

Experimental and quasi-experimental designs often need stable delivery and measurement, while complex or innovative interventions may require adaptive evaluation management.

Quality assurance and early planning help maintain credibility when evaluation requirements change.

## Key Concepts

- Evaluation management
- Flexibility
- Consistency
- Quality assurance

## Source Location

- Section path: 5. Managing an evaluation
- Evidence records: `chunk:magenta-book:magenta-book-5-managing-an-evaluation:c0017`

## Practical Implications

- Build adaptation rules into the evaluation plan.
- Protect comparability when design consistency is required.
- Use quality assurance to manage changes transparently.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that Managing an Evaluation helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## References
